name: Funny-Side-up Pie
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 425°F.
2. Mix flour and salt in a bowl.
3. Cut shortening into the flour mixture using a pastry blender or your fingers until it resembles coarse crumbs.
4. Gradually add cold water, stirring with a fork, until the dough holds together.
5. Divide the dough into two balls. Work with one ball to form a circle larger than an inverted 9-inch pie pan.
6. Transfer the dough to the prepared pie pan and press it evenly around the bottom and sides.
7. Cut bacon into 1 ½-inch pieces and cook until crisp. Drain well.
8. Mix together the soup and sour cream in a bowl, then stir in half of this mixture with the cooked bacon and remaining ingredients.
9. Pour the filling into the pastry-lined pie pan.
10. Roll out the top crust, cut vents to create a "smile," place it over the filling, seal, and flute the edges.
11. Cover the edge with a 1 ½-inch strip of aluminum foil.
12. Bake for 30 minutes.
13. Remove the foil and continue baking until the topping is golden brown, about 15 more minutes.
